summary: German Renaissance painter and leader of the Danube School. Known for The Battle of Alexander at Issus (giant floating tablet, sun and crescent moon), Landscape with Footbridge (one of earliest pure oil landscapes), and St. George Overcoming the Dragon (dwarfed by forest).

works.name: The Battle of Alexander at Issus (1529)
works.description: Massive chaotic battle scene with floating tablet inscription, sun setting over Nile, crescent moon. Commissioned by Duke William IV of Bavaria. 'World landscape' style. Persians in red turbans resembling Turks. Wikipedia

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Leader of the Danube School. Pupil of Lucas Cranach the Elder. Member of the Little Masters (Kleinmeister) alongside Georg Pencz, Heinrich Aldegrever, Beham brothers. Painted four scenes of St. Sebastian for St. Florian Abbey, Rest on the Flight into Egypt (angels in Roman fountain), Nativity of the Virgin (set inside medieval cathedral), Crucifixion with donor portrait, Landscape with a Family of Satyrs. Durer added a lake in his Landauer Altarpiece as homage to Altdorfer. Christopher S. Wood wrote on Altdorfer's influence on origins of landscape genre.

comprehensive_summary:
Albrecht Altdorfer (c. 1480-1538) was a German Renaissance painter and leader of the Danube School. He was also a member of the Little Masters (Kleinmeister), a group of engravers producing detailed postage stamp-sized prints alongside Georg Pencz, Heinrich Aldegrever, and the Beham brothers. He was a pupil of Lucas Cranach the Elder and collaborated with Durer on the monumental woodcut Triumphal Arch. Durer added a lake in his Landauer Altarpiece as an homage to Altdorfer. Christopher S. Wood wrote about Altdorfer's influence on the origins of the landscape genre.

His most famous work is The Battle of Alexander at Issus (1529), a massive battle scene commissioned by Duke William IV of Bavaria after the 1529 Siege of Vienna. It features a giant floating tablet with a Latin inscription by Johannes Aventinus, the sun setting over the Nile, a crescent moon symbolizing the contemporary defeat of Muslims and Suleiman the Magnificent, and the Tower of Babel in the background. The painting unrealistically shows both Cyprus and the Nile. It is painted in the 'world landscape' style pioneered by Joachim Patinir, and the Persians are anachronistically dressed in red turbans and German court dress. The painting is displayed in the Alte Pinakothek alongside four other paintings from the same cycle. Its miniatures are similarly styled to Altdorfer's Triumph of Maximilian and Triumphal Procession, the latter of which he was the second-greatest contributor to.

His Landscape with Footbridge (1520) is considered one of the earliest pure landscapes in Western oil painting, showing a footbridge between a gatehouse and a larch tree. St. George Overcoming the Dragon shows the saint and dragon dwarfed by a gigantic forest.

Altdorfer painted four scenes from the life of St. Sebastian for St. Florian Abbey. His Rest on the Flight into Egypt shows angels playing in a Roman fountain. He set his Nativity of the Virgin unusually inside a medieval cathedral, focusing on architectural details. His Crucifixion includes a donor portrait of a couple kneeling at Jesus' feet. His Landscape with a Family of Satyrs depicts a naked man assaulting a woman in a meadow. He celebrated Regensburg's expulsion of the Jews in 1519.
